title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement [INSERT CONTACT METHOD]. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

previous_headings: Enforcement Guidelines
title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

previous_headings: Enforcement Guidelines
title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

previous_headings: Enforcement Guidelines
title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

previous_headings: Enforcement Guidelines
title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.0, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/0/ code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https:// www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

previous_headings: Tier 1 — Large multi-species datasets, both surfaces confirmed
what: Liu et al. (2024) — Annals of Botany ★★★
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Liu, C., Huang, K., Zhao, Y., Li, Y., & , N. (2024). continental-scale analysis reveals latitudinal gradient stomatal density across amphistomatous species: evolutionary history vs. present-day environment. Annals Botany, 134(5), 877–886. https://doi.org/10.1093/aob/mcae135 high priority: study measured adaxial stomatal density (SDad) abaxial stomatal density (SDab) 486 amphistomatous species–site combinations, belonging 32 plant families, China. large, directly relevant dataset focused specifically leaf surfaces amphistomatous species. Likely traits: adaxial_stomatal_density_mm2, abaxial_stomatal_density_mm2Likely units: check paper — almost certainly mm⁻²Suggested source ID: liu_continental_2024Notes: Check whether species-level means SD provided supplementary table, site-level measurements. paper also reports total stomatal density (SDtotal) stomatal ratio, derived need added. Guard cell length probably reported — confirm.

previous_headings: Tier 1 — Large multi-species datasets, both surfaces confirmed
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Petrík, P., et al. (2024). Linking stomatal size density water use efficiency leaf carbon isotope ratio juvenile mature trees. Physiologia Plantarum, 176, e14619. https://doi.org/10.1111/ppl.14619 promising: study sampled 190 individuals including juvenile mature trees 18 temperate broadleaved tree species, measuring guard cell length stomatal density. study explicitly references adaxial abaxial surfaces tree species. Likely traits: stomatal density, guard cell length — check surfacesSuggested source ID: petrik_linking_2024Notes: paper focuses mature vs. juvenile ontogenetic stages; use data mature trees comparability rest database. surfaces measured, add 18 broadleaved tree species density guard cell length. one surface reported, skip.

previous_headings: Tier 1 — Large multi-species datasets, both surfaces confirmed
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Xiong, D., & Flexas, J. (2020). one side two sides: effects stomatal distribution photosynthesis. New Phytologist, 228, 1754–1766. https://doi.org/10.1111/nph.16801 high priority: Reports adaxial abaxial stomatal density across large number species, adaxial density varying 0 308 stomata mm⁻² adaxial:abaxial density ratio varying 0 0.98. Also measures guard cell length alongside stomatal width calculate stomatal size. Likely traits: adaxial_stomatal_density_mm2, abaxial_stomatal_density_mm2, guard cell lengthSuggested source ID: xiong_one_2020Notes: Check whether species-level means SD/SE provided supplementary table, species means without replication. Confirm guard cell length measurement method comparability database convention.

previous_headings: Tier 2 — Moderate number of species, both surfaces likely
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Zavala-Paez, M., et al. (2026). Climate hybridization shape stomatal trait evolution Populus. New Phytologist. https://doi.org/10.1111/nph.70706 promising: Stomatal size traits including guard cell pore length showed clear associations precipitation across hybrid zone, abaxial guard cells pore lengths varying among genotypes different environments. paper explicitly reports abaxial traits; adaxial traits also likely given stomatal ratio focus. Likely traits: abaxial_stomatal_density_mm2, adaxial_stomatal_density_mm2, guard cell length, pore lengthSuggested source ID: zavalapaez_climate_2026Notes: Populus already database (pallardy_frequency_1979), adds new genotype/population-level coverage. Check whether hybrid zone data can collapsed species-level population-level means, many distinct genotypes/species covered.

previous_headings: Tier 2 — Moderate number of species, both surfaces likely
what: Caseys et al. (2024) — The Plant Journal ★★
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Caseys, C., Muhich, .J., Vega, J., Ahmed, M., Hopper, ., Kelly, D., Kim, S., Madrone, M., Plaziak, T., Wang, M., et al. (2024). Leaf abaxial adaxial surfaces differentially affect interaction Botrytis cinerea across several eudicots. Plant Journal, 120, 1377–1391. promising: study measured adaxial stomatal density 16 plant species part pathogen susceptibility investigation. abaxial stomatal density also measured (likely, given comparative surface focus), give -surface data 16 eudicot species. Likely traits: adaxial_stomatal_density_mm2, abaxial_stomatal_density_mm2Suggested source ID: caseys_leaf_2024Notes: primary focus plant–pathogen interactions, stomatal density surfaces appears measured. Check table supplementary file species-level stomatal density means SD/SE. Guard cell length may reported.

previous_headings: Tier 2 — Moderate number of species, both surfaces likely
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Brench, R.., Wilson, M.J., Thorne, S.J., Fleming, .J., et al. (2026). evolution faster stomata driven increased gas exchange rates rather increasing water use efficiency? New Phytologist. (confirm DOI) promising: Measures stomatal density guard cell length adaxial abaxial surfaces across plant species spanning early-diverging lineages angiosperms (Asterids, Rosids, monocots). Calculates stomatal ratio adaxial:abaxial density. Multi-species evolutionary scope well aligned database. Likely traits: adaxial_stomatal_density_mm2, abaxial_stomatal_density_mm2, guard cell lengthSuggested source ID: brench_evolution_2026Notes: Confirm number species whether per-species means SD/SE available. paper notes early diverging plants often hypostomatous — check amphistomatous species sufficiently represented worth extraction.

previous_headings: Tier 2 — Moderate number of species, both surfaces likely
what: Zhang et al. (2022) — Frontiers in Plant Science ★★
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Zhang, N., Berman, S.R., Joubert, D., et al. (2022). Variation photosynthetic induction major horticultural crops mostly driven differences stomatal traits. Frontiers Plant Science, 13. (confirm DOI) promising: Quantifies stomatal density length adaxial abaxial surfaces across 19 genotypes six horticultural crop species. Stomatal ratios reported highly conserved across crops. Likely traits: adaxial_stomatal_density_mm2, abaxial_stomatal_density_mm2, guard cell lengthSuggested source ID: zhang_variation_2022Notes: Check whether species-level genotype-level means SD/SE available table supplementary file. high conservation stomatal ratios may useful cross-validation existing database entries.

previous_headings: Tier 2 — Moderate number of species, both surfaces likely
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Ayala-Ramos, M.., Montaño-Arias, S.., & Terrazas, T. (2024). Ecological implications stomatal density stomatal index adult stage Mimosa L. (Leguminosae, Caesalpinioideae). (confirm journal DOI) promising: Measures stomatal density (SD), stomatal index (SI), epidermal cell density (ECD), guard cell length (GCL) adaxial abaxial surfaces leaflets seven Mimosa species, correlating traits 21 climate variables. Likely traits: adaxial_stomatal_density_mm2, abaxial_stomatal_density_mm2, guard cell lengthSuggested source ID: ayalaramos_ecological_2024Notes: Small sample (7 species) Mimosa/Leguminosae underrepresented database. M. affinis showed significant differences surfaces — confirm per-species means SD/SE reported surfaces.

previous_headings: Tier 2 — Moderate number of species, both surfaces likely
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Li, Y., et al. (2021). Spatial variation stomatal morphological traits grassland plants Loess Plateau. Ecological Indicators. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ecolind.2021.107560 (confirm DOI) promising: study measured stomatal traits including SDtotal stomatal size across grassland species Loess Plateau, data leaf surfaces amphistomatous species. distribution stomata different surfaces epidermis explicitly reported. Likely traits: stomatal density surfaces, stomatal sizeSuggested source ID: li_spatial_2021Notes: Confirm full citation, DOI, number species. Check whether SDad SDab reported per species supplementary table, SDtotal.

what: Tier 3 — Single crop species, both surfaces — lower priority
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: papers report surfaces single species. add intraspecific variation (among genotypes populations) rather interspecific diversity, lower priority database may still worth adding data easy extract.

previous_headings: Tier 3 — Single crop species, both surfaces — lower priority
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Wall, S., et al. (2022). Stomata abaxial adaxial leaf surfaces contribute differently leaf gas exchange photosynthesis wheat. New Phytologist, 235, 1672–1685. https://doi.org/10.1111/nph.18257 check: study reports stomatal density guard cell length surfaces multiple wheat genotypes. Suggested source ID: wall_stomata_2022Notes: Single species (Triticum aestivum). worth adding clear control-condition genotype mean available replication.

previous_headings: Tier 3 — Single crop species, both surfaces — lower priority
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Samantara, K., et al. (2025). Higher adaxial stomatal density associated lower grain yield spring wheat. New Phytologist. https://doi.org/10.1111/nph.70416 check: study investigated stomatal traits adaxial abaxial leaf surfaces including SD, stomatal ratio, GCL seven spring wheat genotypes field conditions. Suggested source ID: samantara_higher_2025Notes: Single species, seven genotypes. Use non-drought year (2022) control condition. Check whether genotype-level means SD table supplementary file.

what: Lei et al. (2023) — Journal of Experimental Botany
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Lei, Z., , Y., Li, X., , Z., Zhang, Y., et al. (2023). Domestication reduced leaf water use efficiency associated anatomy abaxial stomata cotton. Journal Experimental Botany. (confirm DOI) check: Reports adaxial abaxial stomatal density, stomatal size, pore size, guard cell width wild domesticated cotton genotypes grown field conditions. Multiple genotypes single species. Suggested source ID: lei_domestication_2023Notes: Single species (Gossypium spp.) includes wild domesticated accessions. worth adding genotype-level means SD/SE clearly reported surfaces.

previous_headings: Tier 3 — Single crop species, both surfaces — lower priority
what: Al-Salman et al. (2023) — Plant, Cell & Environment
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Al-Salman, Y., Cano, F.J., Pan, L., Koller, F., et al. (2023). Anatomical drivers stomatal conductance sorghum lines different leaf widths grown different temperatures. Plant, Cell & Environment, 46. (confirm DOI) check: Measures stomatal density adaxial abaxial surfaces sorghum leaves using confocal microscopy; also calculates stomatal size guard cell dimensions. Multiple sorghum lines across temperatures. Suggested source ID: alsalman_anatomical_2023Notes: Single species (Sorghum bicolor). Use control (optimal) temperature condition . Check whether line-level means SD/SE available surfaces.

previous_headings: Tier 3 — Single crop species, both surfaces — lower priority
what: Jensen et al. (2024) — Journal of Experimental Botany
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Bjerring Jensen, N., Vrobel, O., et al. (2024). Stomatal effects ABA metabolism mediate differential regulation leaf flower cooling tomato cultivars exposed heat drought stress. Journal Experimental Botany. (confirm DOI) check: Reports stomatal density length adaxial abaxial leaf surfaces tomato cultivars heat drought stress. Multiple cultivars single species. Suggested source ID: jensen_stomatal_2024Notes: Single species (Solanum lycopersicum). Use control (-stress) condition . Confirms adaxial SD lower abaxial — check cultivar-level means SD/SE available.

previous_headings: Tier 3 — Single crop species, both surfaces — lower priority
what: Chaplin et al. (2026) — Frontiers in Plant Science
title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Full citation: Chaplin, E., Tanaka, E., Merchant, ., Sznajder, B., et al. (2026). QTLs heat-induced stomatal anatomy underpin gas exchange variation field-grown wheat. Frontiers Plant Science. (confirm DOI) check: Measures stomatal density guard cell length (stomatal area proxy) adaxial abaxial surfaces wheat across multiple genotypes multi-environment field trials two years. Suggested source ID: chaplin_qtls_2026Notes: Single species (Triticum aestivum). Use control (non-heat-stressed) time sowing . Stomatal area ranges 1,421–2,602 µm² (abaxial) 1,396–2,661 µm² (adaxial) — confirm comparable guard cell length convention database.

previous_headings: Tier 3 — Single crop species, both surfaces — lower priority

text: Full citation: Driesen, E., De Proft, M., & Saeys, W. (2023). Drought stress triggers alterations adaxial abaxial stomatal development basil leaves increasing water-use efficiency. Horticulture Research. (confirm DOI) check: Measures stomatal density length adaxial abaxial surfaces basil (Ocimum basilicum) control drought conditions. Finds adaxial stomata sensitive water stress. Suggested source ID: driesen_drought_2023Notes: Single species. Use control (well-watered) condition . Lowest priority Tier 3 — worth extracting control-condition data clearly tabulated SD/SE.

title: Candidate Papers (2020–2026)
text: Google Scholar cited-search — look papers cite Muir (2015) Baird et al. (2023) filter 2020–2026; likely study trait space. Plant Stomata Encyclopedia blog (https://plantstomata.wordpress.com) — community-curated index stomatal papers monthly archives; scan titles 2020 onwards multi-species comparative studies. Dryad / Figshare — search datasets containing “stomatal density” “adaxial”; many recent papers deposit data repositories. Key phrase use searches: "adaxial stomatal density" "abaxial stomatal density" species table 2020..2026

title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: shared Google Sheet called data-template two tabs: data — one row per species × trait × source combination (main data entry tab) metadata — one row per source paper ready enter data, copy template rename using Source Identifier, delete default data, enter new data.

title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: row data tab represents one trait measured one species one paper. columns :

title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: Add one row metadata tab new paper. columns :

title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: trait column must use exactly one names — spelling, underscores, capitalisation matter. Copy paste table avoid errors. paper reports trait list, leave note notes column metadata tab.

title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: Every paper needs short identifier used source column data tab source column metadata tab. format : firstauthor: first author’s last name, lowercase, accents special characters keyword: one memorable lowercase word title (avoid generic words like “leaf” “plant”) year: four-digit publication year Examples: yang_large-scale_2014, pathare_increased_2020, baird_how_2023 two papers produce identifier, add -1 second one (e.g., stenglein_leaf_2003 stenglein_leaf_2003-1).

title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: paper worth entering : Reports stomatal traits leaf surfaces (adaxial abaxial) across multiple species data table supplementary file (figures) Reports least means sample sizes — SD SE bonus Measured traits ambient control conditions, part treatment See Candidate Papers list pre-screened suggestions. starting paper, check source identifier already exist metadata tab.

previous_headings: 6. Selecting a Paper
title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: Many papers vary CO₂, water, light, conditions. can still enter data papers, control/ambient group. Add note notes column explaining “control” means paper.

title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: values must converted standard units entry — enter raw values paper uses different units. Record original unit paper density_unit length_unit columns metadata tab. preserves provenance even conversion.

title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: Enter species name exactly appears paper, including abbreviations misspellings. update synonyms accepted names — handled later. paper abbreviates genus (e.g., “E. globulus”), write full name.

what: 9. What to Do When Things Are Unclear
title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: Paper reports SE, SD (vice versa): enter value correct column (se sd) leave blank. Check paper’s methods — tables sometimes mislabel . measure variation reported: leave sd se blank; enter mu n . Sample size varies species: enter typical value n row, note variation metadata notes column. Data figure: skip paper unless figure values can read precisely. attempt , note "digitized figure" metadata. Unsure value: enter best reading add comment cell Google Sheets (Insert → Comment) describing uncertainty. leave cell blank reasonable value.

title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: Use sanity check. value enter falls far outside ranges, double-check units original paper proceeding.

title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: moving next paper, check following Google Sheet: trait names copied exactly list Section 4 — typos source value data tab matches source metadata tab exactly adaxial abaxial rows present trait species (paper reports ) density values mm⁻² length values µm source_id matches paper’s spelling species name mu n filled every row metadata tab exactly one row paper density_unit length_unit metadata reflect original units paper

title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: finished entering species paper: Mark paper complete Candidate Papers tracking list (wherever keeping track progress). Send brief message Chris noting: source identifier, number species entered, issues uncertainties flagged.

title: Updating the ropenstomata Database
text: Density units: papers report stomata per cm² one section per mm² another — always check units table header, just methods. Guard cell length vs. pore length: authors use “stomatal length” mean length pore opening, full guard cell. Check methods description diagram measured. SE vs. SD confusion: Mislabelled columns common. reported values seem implausibly small SD (.e., smaller ’d expect biological variation), may actually SE — check methods. Hypostomatous species: species stomata abaxial surface, adaxial rows simply absent — enter zeros adaxial traits. Duplicate species across papers: species two different papers expected fine. delete duplicates.

title: An Open Stomatal Data Set
text: ropenstomata R database stomatal anatomy synthesized primary literature. WARNING: Although data mostly peer-reviewed literature, users carefully inspect data prior analyses discovered suspect data points. noticed two potential errors: Guard cell length measurements Sundberg (1986) seemed implausibly small, including several species 10 μ\mum. Euphorbia caeladenia Siddiqi et al. (1991) exact ab- adaxial stomatal density Huang (2019) states species small guard cell lengths compared species data set Thanks Adam Roddy identifying issues. can report additional issues.

title: Stomatal anatomy — stomata_anatomy
text: data set contains stomatal anatomical characters (density, length, width, pore length, pore width) ab- adaxial leaf surface number species synthesized primary literature.

title: Stomatal anatomy — stomata_anatomy
text: data frame 5842 rows 7 variables: source_id character Taxonomic name give source paper. trait character Description trait. mu double Trait mean. n double Sample size (see stomata_anatomy_metadata information.) sd double Trait standard deviation. source character Source paper identifier. se double Trait standard error.

title: Stomatal anatomy metadata — stomata_anatomy_metadata
text: contains metadata stomata_anatomy

title: Stomatal anatomy metadata — stomata_anatomy_metadata
text: data frame 38 rows 7 variables: source character Source paper identifier. taxa character Description taxa covered source. location character Location trait data located source. cases, raw data provided directly authors. density_unit character Unit used stomatal density. length_unit character Unit used guard cell pore length width. sample_size double Approximate sample size taxon study. notes character Notes understand units sample size data.
